reasoning: All flagged unsafe findings in qwen's report are false positives: They occur in CI/test scripts that aren't executed during AUR build/install. PKGBUILD is safe and doesn't reference these scripts.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged CI/test scripts as security risks despite them not being executed during makepkg operations
coverage_issues: Both models missed reviewing .install hooks (none present) and build scripts referenced in PKGBUILD (meson/ninja are safe)
qwen reviewed irrelevant files (CI scripts) while missing actual build logic
false_positives: container_job_trampoline.sh: eval usage is standard in CI context, not executed during build
weston.sh: Display server launch is test-only, not malicious
kdl.sh: /ci-kdl path is part of test environment, not build process
capture-devcoredump.sh: Debugging tool for CI, not executed in build
baremetal_build.sh: Downloads are for test infrastructure, not package build
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Models should focus on PKGBUILD, install hooks, and actual build scripts over CI/test files
Enhance context awareness: Models must better distinguish between build-time vs test-only execution contexts
Reduce false positives: Train models to recognize safe patterns in CI scripts (eval, display servers)
Improve prompt clarity: Explicitly restate that CI/test files are irrelevant unless referenced in PKGBUILD
Implement coverage validation: Audit tool should verify build scripts are reviewed before considering CI files
